description: x86-64 disassembly utilities backed by Capstone. All addresses are Lua numbers (doubles representing 64-bit pointers). Capstone is initialised lazily on first use; if initialisation fails, functions return an empty table or `nil` rather than raising an error.

functions.name: at
functions.description:
Disassembles up to `count` instructions starting at `address` and returns them as an array. Each element is an instruction table with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `address` | `number` | Virtual address of this instruction. |
| `mnemonic` | `string` | Instruction mnemonic (e.g. `"mov"`, `"call"`). |
| `op_str` | `string` | Operand string as printed by Capstone (e.g. `"rax, qword ptr [rip + 0x1234]"`). |
| `size` | `number` | Byte length of the instruction. |
| `bytes` | `table` | Array of `number` — the raw bytes of the instruction (1-indexed). |
| `operands` | `table` | Array of operand tables (see below). |

Each operand table always contains `type` (`"reg"`, `"imm"`, `"mem"`, or `"unknown"`) and `size` (number of bytes the operand accesses). Additional fields depend on `type`:
- `"reg"`: `reg` (string) — register name.
- `"imm"`: `imm` (number) — immediate value.
- `"mem"`: `base` (string, optional), `index` (string, optional), `scale` (number), `disp` (number).

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of instructions to decode. Defaults to `1`.

returns.description: Array of instruction tables. Empty if Capstone failed to initialise or could not decode any instructions.

functions.name: at_bytes
functions.description: Disassembles as many complete instructions as fit within `byte_count` bytes starting at `address`. Unlike `at`, the limit is measured in bytes rather than instruction count, which pairs naturally with `disasm.size`. Returns an array of instruction tables with the same shape as `at`.

params.name: byte_count
params.description: Maximum number of bytes to consume.

returns.description: Array of instruction tables. Empty if Capstone failed to initialise or could not decode any instructions.

functions.name: pull_global
functions.description: Decodes the single instruction at `address` and, if it is a RIP-relative `mov` of the form `mov <reg>, qword ptr [rip + offset]`, resolves and returns the absolute target address. This is the standard compiler pattern for loading the address of a global variable. Returns `nil` if the instruction is not that pattern, if disassembly failed, or if Capstone could not be initialised.

params.name: address
params.description: Address of the instruction to inspect.

returns.description: Resolved global address, or `nil` if the instruction does not match the expected pattern.

functions.name: pull_all_globals
description: Scans up to `max_size` instructions starting at `address` and collects the resolved target address of every RIP-relative `mov <reg>, qword ptr [rip + offset]` instruction found (the pattern used by the compiler to access global variables).

Scanning stops early if an `INT3` padding byte is encountered (indicating the end of the function body). Unlike `size`, this function does **not** stop at `ret` or `jmp`, because globals can be loaded after such instructions (e.g. into a return register in the function epilogue).
Returns an array of resolved addresses in the order they were found.

params.name: max_size
params.description: Maximum number of instructions to examine. Defaults to `128`.

returns.description: Array of `number` — resolved global addresses in encounter order. Empty if Capstone failed to initialise, no globals were found, or disassembly produced no instructions.

functions.name: pull_calls
description: Scans up to `max_size` instructions starting at `address` and collects the resolved target address of every `call` instruction found.

Both direct (`call imm`) and RIP-relative indirect (`call qword ptr [rip + offset]`) forms are resolved to absolute addresses. Other indirect forms (e.g. `call rax`) are skipped because the target cannot be determined statically.

Scanning stops early at the first `INT3` padding byte. This is useful for finding thunk addresses of virtual-function calls, where hooking the thunk's inner target is more practical than hooking the thunk itself.
Returns an array of resolved call-target addresses in encounter order.

params.name: max_size
params.description: Maximum number of instructions to examine. Defaults to `128`.

returns.description: Array of `number` — resolved call-target addresses in encounter order. Empty if Capstone failed to initialise, no resolvable calls were found, or disassembly produced no instructions.

functions.name: size
description: Returns the byte size of the function that contains `address`.
**Primary method:** `RtlLookupFunctionEntry` reads the `.pdata` `RUNTIME_FUNCTION` table, which covers all non-leaf functions on x86-64 Windows.
**Fallback:** For leaf functions (which have no `.pdata` entry), Capstone linearly sweeps up to `0x1000` bytes and stops at the first run of `INT3` padding bytes.

A lone `INT3` surrounded by non-`INT3` instructions is treated as a live `__debugbreak()` or assertion trap, not as padding — only a `INT3` immediately after a `ret`/`jmp`, or two or more consecutive `INT3`s, triggers the boundary cut.
Returns `nil` if neither method produces a result.

params.name: address
params.description: Any address within the function to measure (typically the function entry point).

returns.description: Size of the function in bytes, or `nil` if it could not be determined.

functions.name: autosig
functions.description: Copies `size` bytes from `address` and auto-masks them into a byte-pattern signature string with wildcard (`?`) bytes in place of relocatable values (e.g. relative offsets in call/jmp instructions). The resulting string is suitable for use with pattern scanners. Returns `nil` if the masking process produces an empty signature.

params.name: address
params.description: Starting address of the region to signature.

params.name: size
params.description: Number of bytes to read and mask.

returns.description: The auto-generated signature string, or `nil` if one could not be produced.
